summary: Added a lightweight Vim configuration under tools/vimrc to improve local editing ergonomics without enforcing project formatting. The final file adds an 81-column marker, C/Doxygen filetype and syntax handling for .h/.c/.dox files, and appends src to Vim's search path. Review feedback discouraged duplicating indentation settings because the project already had .editorconfig support and editorconfig-vim could apply the correct per-file indentation automatically.

key_files.purpose: Project-local Vim helper configuration for visual column guide, C/Doxygen syntax handling, and src path lookup.

what_changed: Created tools/vimrc as a new 11-line Vim configuration file.
Configured `set colorcolumn=81` with `highlight colorcolumn ctermbg=darkgray` to make the 81st column visually apparent.

Added an `augroup project` with `autocmd BufRead,BufNewFile *.h,*.c,*.dox set filetype=c | set syntax=c.doxygen` so Vim treats project C headers, C sources, and Doxygen files as C with Doxygen syntax.
Appended `src,` to Vim's `path` option via `let &path.="src,"`, helping Vim find files under the source tree.
Indentation settings originally discussed in review were not present in the final diff excerpt, consistent with reviewer advice to rely on .editorconfig/editorconfig-vim instead.
smart_moves: Kept the Vim setup non-enforcing and editor-local, matching the PR description: "Doesn't enforce anything, just makes Vim look better."
Avoided hard-coding indentation policy in the final tools/vimrc after review pointed to the existing .editorconfig as the canonical source for indentation behavior.
Used an autocmd group with `autocmd!`, which makes the configuration reload-safe by clearing prior commands in the same group before redefining them.
Targeted C and Doxygen editing ergonomics directly, which is useful for a C decompilation project without changing build or source behavior.
decomp_lessons: Editor helper files are useful when they improve navigation and syntax visibility, but formatting policy should remain centralized in project-wide tooling like .editorconfig.
For decompilation repositories with mixed file types, avoid a single hard-coded indent rule in editor-specific config because files such as .yml and .clang-format may require different indentation.
A visual 81-column marker can support style awareness while remaining advisory rather than mechanically enforcing formatting.
Vim filetype/syntax autocmds for .c, .h, and .dox files can make decomp source and documentation easier to read without affecting matching or generated output.
naming_conventions: The added Vim autocmd group was named `project`, a generic project-local grouping name.

The file was stored as `tools/vimrc` rather than a root `.vimrc` in the final changed-files list, despite review excerpts referring to `.vimrc`; the available slice does not explain the rename/path difference.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Recommended removing explicit Vim indentation settings and using editorconfig-vim instead, because the repository already had a .editorconfig and editorconfig-vim would also apply correct 2-space indentation for .yml and .clang-format files.
review_feedback.status_from_diff: Appears addressed in the final diff excerpt because tabstop, softtabstop, shiftwidth, and noexpandtab settings are absent.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the editorconfig-vim suggestion with: "Thanks, I'll look into it."

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify tools/vimrc or add instructions for sourcing it from a local Vim configuration.
Search for project decisions around .editorconfig as the canonical formatting source.
Check whether editor-specific config files were later added for other editors and whether they avoided duplicating formatting rules.
